mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline or persistently near-zero federal funds rate lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, mechanically supporting valuations and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days regardless of other conditions.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS level/trend and 20-day forward SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate rises above a crisis-level threshold, it signals that labor market damage has peaked and mean-reversion expectations dominate, causing forward-looking equity markets to rally over the subsequent quarter as investors price in recovery.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE snapshot value 9.7 at decision time
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns are negative or statistically indistinguishable from unconditional baseline in more than 50% of historical instances where UNRATE exceeded 9.0%.

mechanism_narrative: Joint acceleration in retail sales (RSAFS) and nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates simultaneous strengthening of consumer spending and labor income, a combined demand-side signal that predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month more reliably than either series alone.
failure_condition: The joint-condition subsample shows no statistically significant improvement in mean 20-day forward return or Rank IC versus either single-series signal in isolation across a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: During a post-shock recovery regime, a positive month-over-month rebound in industrial production (INDPRO) signals resumption of supply-side capacity utilization, which forward markets reward with sustained positive equity returns over a quarterly horizon as production normalization feeds through to corporate earnings.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO snapshot value 101.61 at decision time
failure_condition: In non-recovery regimes (e.g., stable expansion or ongoing contraction), the same INDPRO rebound trigger produces no significant positive return differential, or produces significant negative returns in the recovery regime itself, invalidating the regime dependency.

mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in housing starts (HOUST) reflects improving consumer confidence and interest-rate-sensitive demand, acting as a leading indicator that translates into positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week as the signal is gradually incorporated into rate-sensitive sector pricing.
failure_condition: Correlation between HOUST month-over-month change and 5-day forward SPX returns is not statistically distinguishable from zero (p > 0.10) across the full sample period.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month acceleration in the CPI index above a defined threshold raises near-term rate-hike expectations, causing an immediate negative repricing of equities over the following trading week as investors anticipate tighter future monetary policy.
failure_condition: Instances of CPIAUCSL MoM change exceeding 0.5% are followed by non-negative mean 5-day SPX returns in more than 55% of historical occurrences.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is near the zero lower bound while unemployment remains elevated, the joint condition signals an entrenched policy-accommodation regime in which sustained monetary stimulus combined with labor slack supports risk asset valuations over a quarterly horizon, an effect stronger than either factor alone.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS snapshot 0.16 and UNRATE snapshot 9.7 at decision time
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns conditional on the joint FEDFUNDS/UNRATE trigger are not significantly different from the unconditional 60-day return distribution (t-test p > 0.05) across historical zero-rate episodes.

mechanism_narrative: During an early recovery regime following a labor market shock, a positive month-over-month acceleration in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) signals renewed hiring momentum that is rapidly priced into equities, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the following trading week.
failure_condition: In the early-recovery regime subsample, positive PAYEMS month-over-month change fails to produce mean 5-day forward returns significantly greater than zero (p > 0.10).
